text.title: Synthetic Users Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs a concrete symptom with sourced root-cause and a concrete fix the author can apply.

antipattern.symptom: Stakeholder presentation cites '67% of users said X' without flagging the data as synthetic.
antipattern.root-cause: Synthetic flag dropped during slide copy-paste.
antipattern.fix: Enforce `synthetic: true` column at validator level; block exports missing the flag.

antipattern.symptom: Pricing committee uses synthetic survey to pick a $/mo number; live customers churn at the chosen tier.
antipattern.root-cause: Synthetic willingness-to-pay treated as real demand signal.
antipattern.fix: Reject synthetic data for any pricing decision; require live customer panel.

antipattern.symptom: Synthetic panel of 100 'said yes' to every probe; product ships and crashes on first real edge case.
antipattern.root-cause: Adversarial sampling skipped to save tokens.
antipattern.fix: Enforce 30% adversarial minimum at generator level.

antipattern.symptom: Persona reads like a marketing avatar: 'Sarah, 32, busy mom'; produces zero useful variance.
antipattern.fix: Require ≥3 cited evidence anchors per persona before generation.
